body.title: [sprint] Inference: a candidate raising a hard error falls through to the next tier candidate
body.intention: I intend that when a routed candidate raises a HARD error (e.g. Gemini 404 / key-not-set), dispatch advances to the next available tier candidate instead of aborting the whole request, so one dead provider doesn't sink a servable request.
body.description:
**Split from T-inference-resolve-requests-by-tier (#2 of 3).** Today route() returns ONE decision; if source.call() raises a hard error, dispatch propagates it and the request dies even though lower-priority tier candidates are live.

**Shape:** Let dispatch try tier candidates in priority order — either route() exposes ranked candidates (route_candidates(task_class)) and dispatch loops, or a re-route-excluding-failed loop around source.call(). A hard error (4xx/auth/model-not-found — reuse _classify_ping_failure categories) advances to the next candidate; only when all candidates are exhausted does the complete-inference-failure chokepoint fire (no-provider:<task_class> alarm, already shipped in #1).
**Affected files:** devices/inference/device.py (dispatch loop), devices/inference/rules_engine.py (ranked candidates).
**Design rules:** inference-proxy-only; pure router preserved; fail-LOUD only when ALL candidates exhausted.
**Scope boundary:** IN — hard-error classification + fall-through to next candidate + exhaustion lands on the existing chokepoint. OUT — empty-response fall-through (T-inference-empty-response-fallthrough); the #1 dead-path (shipped); honest ollama liveness (#3).
**Test plan:** inject two fake candidates for one tier where the higher-priority one raises a hard error on call(); assert dispatch returns the lower-priority candidate's text; assert all-candidates-hard-error lands on the no-provider alarm + clean error.
**Completion criteria:** a tier with a hard-erroring top candidate and a live lower candidate returns the live candidate's text; all-candidates-failing raises the no-provider alarm + clean error; non-hard errors are unaffected.

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] datacenter_logs/<device>/<subsystem>/ is the log hierarchy. Never write to a flat — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] PATTERN-001: BaseDevice / BaseShim Lifecycle: `rollback()` must be idempotent — safe to call even if — applies: tags: pattern, pattern-001 (source: docs/design_patterns_inventory.md#PATTERN-001: BaseDevice / BaseShim Lifecycle)
…and 38 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set

**[2026-07-01] Load-bearing for the shipped escalation driver (already pri 0.55):** the DS availability retry (T-router-failure-bump-escalation) re-routes through the selector, which can re-pick the SAME source that just raised (health not marked unavailable synchronously) — burning retries on a dead source. This intra-dispatch candidate fall-through (exclude the failed source, try next) is what makes the availability retry PROGRESS. Shares the TRIED-SET mechanism with T-inference-escalation-ladder-family-then-architect — build the tried-set once, both consume it.
